2605.07806.title: Beyond Confidence: Rethinking Self-Assessments for Performance Prediction in LLMs
2605.07806.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in settings where reliable self-assessment is critical. Assessing model reliability has evolved from using probabilistic correctness estimates to, more recently, eliciting verbalized confidence. Confidence, however, has been shown to be an inconsistent and overoptimistic predictor of model correctness. Drawing on cognitive appraisal theory, a framework from human psychology that decomposes self-evaluation into multiple components, we propose a multidimensional perspective on model self-assessment. We elicit six appraisal-based dimensions of self-assessment, alongside confidence, and evaluate their utility for predicting model failure across 12 LLMs and 38 tasks spanning eight domains. We find that competence-related appraisal dimensions, particularly effort and ability, consistently match or outperform confidence across most settings. Effort additionally yields less overoptimistic estimates that remain stable across model sizes. In contrast, affective dimensions provide marginally predictive signals. Furthermore, the most informative dimension varies systematically with task characteristics: effort is most predictive for reasoning-intensive tasks, while ability and confidence dominate on retrieval-oriented tasks. Broadly, our findings indicate that structured multidimensional self-assessment is a promising approach to improving the reliability and safety of language model deployment across diverse real-world settings.
2605.07806.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在可靠的自我評估至關重要的環境中被越來越多地使用。評估模型的可靠性已經從使用概率正確性估計演變為最近的口頭信心引導。然而，信心已被證明是一個不一致且過於樂觀的模型正確性預測指標。根據認知評價理論，這是一種將自我評估分解為多個組件的人類心理學框架，我們提出了一種對模型自我評估的多維度視角。我們引出了六個基於評價的自我評估維度，並與信心一起評估它們在預測12個LLM和涵蓋八個領域的38個任務中的模型失敗的效用。我們發現，與能力相關的評價維度，特別是努力和能力，在大多數環境中始終與信心相匹配或超越。努力還產生了較少的過於樂觀的估計，並在不同模型大小中保持穩定。相比之下，情感維度提供的預測信號邊際。 此外，最具信息量的維度與任務特徵系統性地變化：對於推理密集型任務，努力是最具預測性的，而能力和信心在檢索導向任務中佔主導地位。總體而言，我們的研究結果表明，結構化的多維度自我評估是一種有前景的方法，可以改善語言模型在各種現實世界環境中的可靠性和安全性。
2605.07806.author: Sree Bhattacharyya et.al.
2605.07806.authors: Sree Bhattacharyya, Samarth Khanna, Leona Chen, Lucas Craig, Tharun Dilliraj, James Z. Wang
